_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-1 12-Lead ECG (Revised 3/31/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-1: 12-Lead ECG — MDHHS Approved 3/31/2025
purpose: Establish standardized acquisition, interpretation, and transmission of 12-lead ECG for patients with potential cardiac conditions. MCA determines which provider levels may obtain and transmit 12-lead ECGs.

sections.title: Indications for 12-Lead ECG
points: Chest pain or pressure
Abdominal pain
Syncope
Shortness of breath
Pain or discomfort often associated with cardiac ischemia, such as jaw, neck, shoulder, left arm, or other presentations, unless the cause can be identified as traumatic or musculoskeletal
If there is doubt about pain/discomfort origin or the presentation seems atypical for the mechanism, perform a 12-lead
Nausea, vomiting, diaphoresis, dizziness, or patient expression of feelings of doom when the illness etiology suggests acute coronary syndrome or is indeterminate
Clinical judgment of the paramedic even in the absence of the listed signs/symptoms

sections.title: 12-Lead ECG Acquisition
points: Obtain 12-lead ECG per MCA selection — scope varies by MCA (Paramedic, Specialist, or EMT)
Place leads in standard anatomical positions
Minimize patient movement and artifact during acquisition
Repeat ECG every 5–10 minutes or with any change in patient condition
If ST elevation ≥ 1 mm in two or more contiguous leads: relay to receiving hospital immediately (STEMI relay)
If chest pain with left bundle branch block is present: relay to receiving hospital immediately
If ST elevation noted in II, III, aVF: obtain right-sided leads — place V4 on right side of chest at same position (V4r) to evaluate for right ventricular MI
If ST depression noted in V1–V4 with ACS symptoms: obtain posterior leads with at least two posterior leads; V4 becomes V7, V5 becomes V8, and V6 becomes V9
If hospital requests interpretation assistance: relay ECG

sections.title: STEMI Relay Report Elements
points: Report 'STEMI Suspected' or equivalent machine indication of acute MI
Location of MI, such as 'ST elevation, consider _____ injury'
Time of onset of chest pain if present
Current level of pain
Cardiac history, including previous MI, CHF, CABG, angioplasty, or stent
Possible false-positive indicators, including tachyarrhythmia, left bundle branch block, pacemaker, wide QRS, or artifact after a previous negative ECG
Relay ECG to receiving facility if transmission equipment is available per MCA selection

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select which provider levels are authorized to obtain a 12-lead ECG
MCA must select which provider levels are authorized to transmit a 12-lead ECG to the receiving facility

deterioration_flags: ST elevation ≥ 1 mm in 2 or more contiguous leads — STEMI relay immediately; activate MCA STEMI transport protocol
Chest pain with left bundle branch block — relay to receiving hospital as soon as possible
ST depression V1–V4 with ACS symptoms — obtain posterior leads to rule out posterior MI
ST elevation II, III, aVF — obtain right-sided V4r to rule out right ventricular MI
Hemodynamic instability with ECG changes — do not delay transport for repeated ECGs
scope_notes: 12-Lead ECG acquisition: per MCA selection (Paramedic only / Specialist and above / EMT and above)
12-Lead ECG transmission: per MCA selection (Paramedic only / Specialist and above / EMT and above)
Interpretation and relay: provider performing acquisition; contact Medical Control as needed
STEMI transport destination: per MCA STEMI transport protocol
